title: Invariant Testing, Fuzzing, and Reproducible Benchmarks · Complexity Forensics
summary: Audit custom structures whose happy paths pass but long random operation sequences corrupt state. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a model-based test and benchmark harness and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write executable representation invariants that fail near the corrupting operation.
Compare custom operations against a simpler trusted reference model.
Generate reproducible operation sequences and retain seeds for failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
